name: AnonInnerLength
description: Checks for long anonymous inner classes. Rationale: If an anonymous inner class becomes very long it is hard to understand and to see the flow of the method where the class is defined. Therefore, long anonymous inner classes should usually be refactored into a named inner class. See also Bloch, Effective Java, p. 93.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of lines allowed.

name: ExecutableStatementCount
description: Restricts the number of executable statements to a specified limit.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum threshold allowed.

options.name: tokens
options.description: tokens to check

name: FileLength
description: Checks for long source files. Rationale: If a source file becomes very long it is hard to understand. Therefore, long classes should usually be refactored into several individual classes that focus on a specific task.

options.name: fileExtensions
options.description: Specify the file extensions of the files to process.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of lines allowed.

name: LambdaBodyLength
description: Checks lambda body length. Rationale: Similar to anonymous inner classes, if lambda body becomes very long it is hard to understand and to see the flow of the method where the lambda is defined. Therefore, long lambda body should usually be extracted to method.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of lines allowed.

name: LineLength
description: Checks for long lines. Rationale: Long lines are hard to read in printouts or if developers have limited screen space for the source code, e.g. if the IDE displays additional information like project tree, class hierarchy, etc. - The calculation of the length of a line takes into account the number of expanded spaces for a tab character ("'\t'"). The default number of spaces is "8". To specify a different number of spaces, the user can set "Checker" property "tabWidth" which applies to all Checks, including "LineLength"; or can set property "tabWidth" for "LineLength" alone. - By default, package and import statements (lines matching pattern "^(package|import) .*") are not verified by this check. - Trailing comments are taken into consideration while calculating the line length. import java.util.regex.Pattern; // The length of this comment will be taken into consideration " In the example above the length of the import statement is just 31 characters but total length will be 94 characters.

options.name: fileExtensions
options.description: Specify the file extensions of the files to process.

options.name: ignorePattern
options.description: Specify pattern for lines to ignore.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum line length allowed.

name: MethodCount
description: Checks the number of methods declared in each type declaration by access modifier or total count. This check can be configured to flag classes that define too many methods to prevent the class from getting too complex. Counting can be customized to prevent too many total methods in a type definition ("maxTotal"), or to prevent too many methods of a specific access modifier ("private", "package", "protected" or "public"). Each count is completely separated to customize how many methods of each you want to allow. For example, specifying a "maxTotal" of 10, still means you can prevent more than 0 "maxPackage" methods. A violation won't appear for 8 public methods, but one will appear if there is also 3 private methods or any package-private methods. Methods defined in anonymous classes are not counted towards any totals. Counts only go towards the main type declaration parent, and are kept separate from it's children's inner types. public class ExampleClass { public enum Colors { RED, GREEN, YELLOW; public String getRGB() { ... } // NOT counted towards ExampleClass } public void example() { // counted towards ExampleClass Runnable r = (new Runnable() { public void run() { ... } // NOT counted towards ExampleClass, won't produce any violations }); } public static class InnerExampleClass { protected void example2() { ... } // NOT counted towards ExampleClass, // but counted towards InnerExampleClass } } "

options.name: maxPackage
options.description: Specify the maximum number of package methods allowed.

options.name: maxPrivate
options.description: Specify the maximum number of private methods allowed.

options.name: maxProtected
options.description: Specify the maximum number of protected methods allowed.

options.name: maxPublic
options.description: Specify the maximum number of public methods allowed.

options.name: maxTotal
options.description: Specify the maximum number of methods allowed at all scope levels.

options.name: tokens
options.description: tokens to check

name: MethodLength
description: Checks for long methods and constructors. Rationale: If a method becomes very long it is hard to understand. Therefore, long methods should usually be refactored into several individual methods that focus on a specific task.

options.name: countEmpty
options.description: Control whether to count empty lines and comments.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of lines allowed.

options.name: tokens
options.description: tokens to check

name: OuterTypeNumber
description: Checks for the number of types declared at the outer (or root) level in a file. Rationale: It is considered good practice to only define one outer type per file.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of outer types allowed.

name: ParameterNumber
description: Checks the number of parameters of a method or constructor.

options.name: ignoreAnnotatedBy
options.description: Ignore methods and constructors annotated with the specified annotation(s).

options.name: ignoreOverriddenMethods
options.description: Ignore number of parameters for methods with @Override annotation.

options.name: max
options.description: Specify the maximum number of parameters allowed.

options.name: tokens
options.description: tokens to check

name: RecordComponentNumber
description: Checks the number of record components in the header of a record definition.

options.name: accessModifiers
options.description:
Access modifiers of record definitions where the number of record
 components should be checked.

options.name: max
options.description:
Specify the maximum number of components allowed in the header
 of a record definition.
